Name: STREAMS
LongName: STREAMS guidelines: standards for technical reporting in environmental and host-associated microbiome studies
Description: STREAMS provides technical reporting standards for environmental, synthetic, and non-human host-associated microbiome studies, extending STORMS toward broader microbiome domains and machine-actionable reporting.
Aim: To improve completeness, standardization, and FAIR-aligned technical reporting in environmental and host-associated microbiome research, including metadata, multi-omics methods, controls, analyses, and data/code access.

Scope.Coverage: Microbiome manuscripts involving environmental, synthetic, or non-human host-associated systems with sequencing and/or other omics data generation, analysis, and reporting requirements across metadata, methods, and access sections.
Scope.Focus: Structured technical reporting of study design, sample/environment context, collection and processing methods, controls, omics generation metadata, bioinformatics/statistical analysis, bias and sensitivity assessment, and data/code/metadata accessibility.
Scope.ContrastWith: Not a primary study-design guideline for randomized trials, observational epidemiology, or systematic reviews. STREAMS is a microbiome technical-reporting overlay and is not intended for non-microbiome papers that merely mention streams (for example hydrology, media streaming, or network traffic).

primary.description: Kelliher JL, Aiewsakun P, Brislawn C, et al. STREAMS guidelines: standards for technical reporting in environmental and host-associated microbiome studies. Nature Microbiology. 2025;10:3059-3068.

items.item_question: Is the abstract organized to communicate key STREAMS elements?
items.item_question_background: Abstracts are often reused across systems and should stand alone as complete summaries.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract whether the abstract structure covers design, methods, analyses, and results in a concise, reproducible format.

items.item_question: Does the abstract summarize the study design?
items.item_question_background: Design context supports correct interpretation and indexing.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract study design language in the abstract.

items.item_question: Does the abstract identify environment/host and sample context?
items.item_question_background: Sample and ecosystem context are essential in microbiome studies.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract sample/environment/host descriptors from the abstract.

items.item_question: For host-associated studies, does the abstract include host information?
items.item_question_background: Host context affects interpretability for host-associated microbiomes.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract host descriptors (species, relevant host attributes) when applicable.

items.item_question: Does the abstract summarize methods and analyses, including omics approach?
items.item_question_background: Concise methods language improves discoverability and appraisal.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract major laboratory and analytical methods (for example sequencing/omics and analysis approach).

items.item_question: Does the abstract report principal findings and their significance?
items.item_question_background: Abstract-level findings should reflect central technical outcomes.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract primary reported outcomes and high-level interpretation in the abstract.

items.item_question: Is background and rationale provided in context of prior microbiome work?
items.item_question_background: Contextual rationale anchors technical reporting relevance.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract background synthesis, prior evidence, and rationale for this microbiome study.

items.item_question: Are hypotheses/objectives/research questions explicitly stated?
items.item_question_background: Explicit questions guide methods and analysis choices.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract stated hypotheses, objectives, and/or research questions.

items.item_question: Is overall study design explicitly reported?
items.item_question_background: Design details are required for reproducibility and review.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract study design type, including whether data reuse, combined analyses, or meta-analyses were used.

items.item_question: Are sample types and core metadata described?
items.item_question_background: Sample metadata quality determines downstream reuse.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract sample types, sample descriptors, and any SynCom/community construction information.

items.item_question: Is environmental/geographic context reported with sufficient precision?
items.item_question_background: Location/context metadata are central for ecological interpretability.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract environment ontology/context details and geographic location information (including coordinates when provided).

items.item_question: Are collection dates and temporal sampling context reported?
items.item_question_background: Temporal factors influence microbiome composition and comparability.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract collection dates, duration, frequency, and seasonality/temporal factors.

items.item_question: For host-associated studies, are host details sufficiently described?
items.item_question_background: Host metadata are required for non-human host-associated microbiome interpretation.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract host species and relevant host metadata.

items.item_question: Are ethics and permitting details documented?
items.item_question_background: Permits and ethical governance are critical for responsible sampling.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract permits, permissions, ethics approvals, and any data governance statements.

items.item_question: Are study treatments/conditions and context clearly described?
items.item_question_background: Treatments and conditions can alter microbiome outcomes.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract all treatment/condition details for sampled hosts/environments and experimental context.

items.item_question: Are sample collection methods and protocols fully documented?
items.item_question_background: Collection methods influence contamination risk and comparability.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract collection protocols, tools, body site/niche details, and referenced SOPs.

items.item_question: Are inclusion/exclusion criteria for samples reported?
items.item_question_background: Selection criteria affect representativeness and bias.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract sample eligibility and selection criteria used before analysis.

items.item_question: Is final analytic sample size clearly stated?
items.item_question_background: Final sample size is needed for analytical appraisal.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract final number of samples used in analysis and any attrition details.

items.item_question: Are sample storage and preservation conditions reported?
items.item_question_background: Storage conditions can alter measured microbial signals.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract storage media, temperature, preservation methods, and holding times.

items.item_question: Are transport/shipping conditions and handling described?
items.item_question_background: Transit handling can introduce technical variation.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract transportation/shipping conditions and chain-of-custody handling details.

items.item_question: Are extraction methods and protocols clearly reported?
items.item_question_background: Extraction protocols strongly influence downstream comparability.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract extraction methods, kits, protocol identifiers, and deviations from established procedures.

items.item_question: Are sample processing steps and experimental manipulations documented?
items.item_question_background: Processing details are needed to interpret technical effects.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract all processing, manipulation, and culturing details before omics generation.

items.item_question: Are nucleic acid library preparation methods fully specified?
items.item_question_background: Library preparation metadata are essential for repository compliance and reproducibility.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract library prep protocols, kits, adapters/indexing details, and sequencing-prep metadata.

items.item_question: Are enrichment/depletion methods and rationale reported?
items.item_question_background: Enrichment/depletion can bias observed community composition.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract enrichment/depletion procedures and rationale, including expected biases.

items.item_question: When amplification is used, are targets and primers reported?
items.item_question_background: Target region and primer details are critical for interpretability and comparability.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract amplified target region(s), primer sequences, and amplification conditions when applicable.

items.item_question: Are positive controls and their placement in workflow described?
items.item_question_background: Positive controls support technical quality assessment.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract positive control type(s), where used, and performance outcomes.

items.item_question: Are negative controls and contamination checks described?
items.item_question_background: Negative controls are crucial for contamination assessment.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract negative control type(s), where used, and contamination findings/handling.

items.item_question: Are quantity/quality assessments of samples and analytes reported?
items.item_question_background: Quality metrics are needed for reliability and comparability.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract concentration/quality metrics and QC methods for samples, nucleic acids, proteins, and metabolites.

items.item_question: Are laboratory contamination mitigation strategies documented?
items.item_question_background: Contamination controls are central to microbiome data trustworthiness.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract contamination prevention and detection strategies at each relevant workflow stage.

items.item_question: Are biological and technical replicates and their usage reported?
items.item_question_background: Replicate design impacts robustness and inference.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract replicate types, counts, rationale, and how they were handled in analysis.

items.item_question: Are sequencing generation details sufficiently reported?
items.item_question_background: Sequencing platform metadata are needed for technical reproducibility.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract sequencing platforms, chemistries, run settings, and relevant submission metadata.

items.item_question: For MS data, are generation settings reported?
items.item_question_background: MS metadata are required for reproducibility of metabolomics/proteomics analyses.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract mass spectrometry instrumentation/settings and processing metadata where applicable.

items.item_question: Are linked/contextual datasets and their relation to study data described?
items.item_question_background: Linked data context supports integrated interpretation and reuse.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract other datasets used (environmental, host, or contextual) and how they were linked.

items.item_question: Are anticipated/observed batch effects and handling reported?
items.item_question_background: Batch effects can distort biological conclusions if unaddressed.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract sources of batch effects and any mitigation/adjustment strategies.

items.item_question: Are bioinformatics workflows and parameters fully reported?
items.item_question_background: Workflow transparency is essential for computational reproducibility.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract full workflow steps, tools, versions, and key parameters.

items.item_name: Data quality filtering and control performance
items.item_question: Are quality filtering criteria and control performance reported?
items.item_question_background: QC filtering decisions strongly influence final data products.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract filtering thresholds and outcomes, including positive/negative control behavior.

items.item_question: Are normalization methods and rationale reported?
items.item_question_background: Normalization choices affect comparability and effect estimates.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract normalization/transformation methods and rationale.

items.item_question: Are taxonomy/annotation database names and versions fully specified?
items.item_question_background: Database versions are critical to reproducibility and interpretation.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract database names, versions, access dates/links/DOIs, and custom database construction details.

items.item_question: Are statistical methods and transformations comprehensively reported?
items.item_question_background: Statistical transparency supports robust interpretation and reanalysis.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract statistical tests/models, transformations, and rationale for method choices.

items.item_question: Is handling of missing information/data reported?
items.item_question_background: Missingness handling can materially affect results.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract missing data definitions, handling strategy, and exclusions due to missingness.

items.item_question: Are potential biases/confounding factors and mitigation described?
items.item_question_background: Bias/confounding control is required for valid technical interpretation.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract identified biases/confounders and methods used to minimize or adjust for them.

items.item_question: If subgroups are analyzed, are subgroup criteria and rationale reported?
items.item_question_background: Subgroup analysis can be misleading without explicit prespecification and rationale.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract subgroup creation criteria, rationale, and subgroup-specific analysis approach.

items.item_question: Are sensitivity analyses and their setup reported?
items.item_question_background: Sensitivity analyses support robustness assessment.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract sensitivity analyses performed and the factors/assumptions varied.

items.item_question: Are significance thresholds and multiple-testing control methods reported?
items.item_question_background: Thresholding and correction choices affect false positive rates.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract significance thresholds, correction methods (for example FDR), and criteria for statistical calls.

items.item_question: Are metadata standards used and metadata access details reported?
items.item_question_background: Metadata standardization enables interoperability and reuse.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract metadata standards used (for example MIxS/ENVO) and where metadata can be accessed.

items.item_question: When relevant, are host-associated data access details and constraints reported?
items.item_question_background: Host data governance and access constraints affect reproducibility and ethics.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract host-associated data availability, access process, and restrictions/justifications.

items.item_question: Are raw omics data access details reported?
items.item_question_background: Raw data deposition is central to FAIR compliance.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract repositories, accession identifiers, and availability status for raw data.

items.item_question: Are processed/derived data availability details reported?
items.item_question_background: Processed data access supports direct reuse and verification.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract links/accessions for processed data tables/matrices and documentation.

items.item_question: Are software/code and workflow artifacts made available?
items.item_question_background: Computational reproducibility requires access to code and software details.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract code repositories, software environments/containers, and workflow/pipeline sharing details.

items.item_question: Are any data/code access limitations and justifications clearly stated?
items.item_question_background: Transparent constraints are needed when full openness is not possible.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract stated reasons for restricted access and the pathway for controlled access when available.

items.item_question: Are core results and resulting datasets summarized clearly?
items.item_question_background: Results summaries should connect methods to interpretable outcomes.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract core reported data summaries and key output structures.

items.item_question: Is sample/analysis flow and counting transparently reported?
items.item_question_background: Flow transparency helps identify attrition and filtering effects.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract counts through each major processing/analysis stage.

items.item_question: Are control performance and quality outcomes reported?
items.item_question_background: Control outcomes contextualize confidence in biological findings.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract reported control behavior and quality-control outcomes.

items.item_question: Are major microbiome findings reported with sufficient technical context?
items.item_question_background: Findings should be traceable to the technical pipeline and data.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract principal microbiome findings and the technical context used to support them.

items.item_question: Do figures/tables adequately communicate methods-linked results?
items.item_question_background: Figure/table completeness improves independent interpretation.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract key figure/table elements and whether captions/labels allow independent understanding.

items.item_question: Are effect estimates and uncertainty measures reported where applicable?
items.item_question_background: Uncertainty reporting supports proper technical interpretation.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract effect sizes, confidence intervals, and uncertainty/statistical summaries where provided.

items.item_question: Are subgroup/sensitivity result outputs reported when analyses were performed?
items.item_question_background: Supplementary analyses should be reported with comparable detail.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract subgroup and sensitivity results and how they compare to primary findings.

items.item_question: Are supporting supplementary outputs linked clearly to reported results?
items.item_question_background: Linking main and supplementary outputs enables traceability.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract references to supplementary tables/files and how they support reported results.

items.item_question: Are key findings interpreted in line with methods and evidence presented?
items.item_question_background: Interpretation should be consistent with technical and analytical evidence.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract how authors interpret central findings in relation to objectives and methods.

items.item_question: Are findings contextualized against existing literature and datasets?
items.item_question_background: Positioning against prior work supports synthesis and reuse.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract comparisons of findings with prior studies and references.

items.item_question: Are limitations and potential biases discussed transparently?
items.item_question_background: Limitations and biases are critical for responsible interpretation.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract discussed technical and analytical limitations, and potential bias/confounding sources.

items.item_question: Is generalisability/applicability of findings discussed?
items.item_question_background: Applicability context supports translation across systems and studies.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract statements on transferability/generalisability across environments, hosts, or systems.

items.item_name: Implications for environmental and host-associated microbiome science
items.item_question: Are practical/research implications for microbiome science articulated?
items.item_question_background: Implication statements help connect technical reporting to broader impact.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract stated implications for environmental, synthetic, and/or host-associated microbiome research.

items.item_question: Are conclusions and future directions clearly stated?
items.item_question_background: Conclusions and next steps aid interpretation and reuse planning.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract final conclusions and proposed future work.

items.item_question: Are acknowledgements and contributor context provided?
items.item_question_background: Acknowledgements can clarify resource and collaboration context.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract acknowledgements and relevant consortium/contributor statements.

items.item_question: Are funding sources and support statements provided?
items.item_question_background: Funding transparency supports assessment of study context.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract all funding/support disclosures.

items.item_question: Are competing interests/conflicts disclosed?
items.item_question_background: Conflict transparency is essential for trust and appraisal.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract conflict-of-interest or competing-interest disclosures.

items.item_question: Are supplementary materials clearly identified and linked?
items.item_question_background: Supplementary resources often contain key technical details.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract supplementary material references and where they can be accessed.

items.item_question: Are data and code availability statements complete and actionable?
items.item_question_background: Clear availability statements are needed for machine-actionable reuse.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract repository locations/accessions and conditions for data and code reuse.

Aliases: standards for technical reporting in environmental and host-associated microbiome studies
Strengthening the Organization and Reporting of Microbiome Studies
EvidenceExamplesPositive: We profiled soil microbiomes using shotgun metagenomics and report MIxS-compliant metadata, sample collection context, controls, bioinformatics workflow versions, and repository accession identifiers.
This host-associated microbiome study details sample storage, extraction methods, 16S amplification primers, contamination controls, normalization procedures, and code/data availability statements.

Our synthetic community microbiome manuscript reports positive and negative controls, sequencing platform settings, database versions for taxonomy assignment, sensitivity analyses, and FAIR-aligned metadata deposition.
EvidenceExamplesNegative: This hydrology paper analyzes streamflow forecasting and watershed discharge without microbiome sequencing, metadata standards, or omics workflows.
The manuscript describes a video streaming optimization system and does not involve environmental or host-associated microbiome data generation.
This ecological field report mentions streams and water quality but includes no microbiome profiling, sequencing methods, or microbiome data repositories.
Matching.Explain: Apply as a microbiome discipline overlay for manuscripts generating or analyzing environmental, synthetic, or non-human host-associated microbiome omics data, especially when methods/metadata/repository reporting detail appears in full text even without explicit STREAMS citation.
